_README: Copy this to dispatch-secrets.staging.json or dispatch-secrets.production.json and fill in real values. Populated files are gitignored (`dispatch-secrets.*.json`). Feed to scripts/seed-secrets.sh via `npm run seed:staging` / `npm run seed:production`. Any value containing REPLACE_ME blocks the seed — guard against accidental empty/placeholder deploys. Two fields auto-derive when left empty: web-config.cookiePassword (generated with openssl rand) and grafana-cloud.authHeader (computed from instanceId + apiToken). Every top-level key maps to a Secrets Manager entry at dispatch/{env}/<key>. dispatch/{env}/db-credentials is CDK-managed — do not add it here.
_approvers_hint: WorkOS User Management IDs (user_01...) — NOT Directory Sync IDs (directory_user_01...). cosUserId = Chief of Staff; backupApproverIds = 0+ fallbacks. The record only exists after first AuthKit sign-in; bootstrap yourself via WorkOS dashboard → Authentication → Features → Hosted UI (click the hosted URL, sign in once), then copy your ID from User Management → Users. Changing this is live — API cache is 5 min, no redeploy. Full walkthrough in docs/secrets.md § Getting a WorkOS User Management ID.
_slack_hint: Bot token (xoxb-...) needs exactly four scopes: channels:history (read announcements + team), channels:read (look up channel IDs), chat:write (post to review channel), users:read (resolve user_id → display name). No private-channel scopes, no admin scopes. Channel IDs start with C and are visible in Slack → channel name → View channel details. Bot must be /invite'd to all three channels. Full walkthrough in docs/slack-app-setup.md.
